raw.objective: “To assess whether, using the current regulatory criteria, therapeutically important differences can exist between bioequivalent carbamazepine (CBZ) tablets. A secondary goal was to demonstrate quantitatively the relationship between the risk of neurological adverse effects to orally ingested CBZ and the rate of absorption.”
raw.covariates: Tested: Not specified Included: formulation on Ka and Vd. Concentration on risk of adverse effect.
raw.observations: The risk of developing adverse reactions were modelled using the impact of concentration and time. It appears that due to the differences in absorption kinetics, difference exist between the formulations in risk of developing adverse reactions, for which needs to be adjusted in the PK model. Simply said, absorption kinetics with lower Cmax but similar AUC as others will show a lower risk of developing adverse reactions.
raw.dose: Single oral dose in healthy subjects
raw.equations: Ka= (theta1 / theta2 / theta3 / theta4)*exp(eta1) Vd/F= (theta5 / theta6 / theta7 / theta8 )*exp(eta2) CL/F= theta9*exp(eta3) Cobs=Cipred+eps1 logOR(t)=(theta10+eta4)*log(Conc(t))+(theta11+eta5)*t+(theta12+eta6)
raw.parameter_values: theta1=0.240 theta2=0.825 theta3=1.59 theta4=0.472 theta5=96.4 theta6=68.1 theta7=70.8 theta8=83.5 theta9=1.35 theta10=1.00 theta11=-0.29 theta12=-0.83 omega1=? omega2=? omega3=? omega4=? omega5=? omega6=? sigma1=?

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
